name: Austrade Landing Pads
city_region: San Francisco, Singapore, Berlin and partner hubs
status: Recurring government program; apply anytime, cohorts and hub availability vary
opens: Rolling intake, verify current hub availability at source
eligibility: Australian market-ready startups and scaleups seeking international expansion
au_eligible: Yes, designed specifically for Australian founders
requires_local: No local entity needed to apply; you operate temporarily from the host hub
stage: Market-ready / early-scale (some tolerance for strong pre-revenue)
funding_amount: In-kind (no cash grant): desk space, mentoring, market introductions
other_benefits: Coworking in the host hub, market-specific mentoring, investor and customer introductions, soft-landing
key_documents: Business overview, traction summary, expansion plan, market rationale
project_match: Aura of Intelligence, civic-tech, XR, health tech expansion
why_relevant: Purpose-built for an Australian founder who wants to test overseas markets without relocating permanently or incorporating abroad. Lowest-friction on-ramp to global hubs.
risks: In-kind only, no capital; competitive; expects some commercial readiness rather than pure concept stage
verify: Confirm which hubs are currently accepting and current eligibility bar

pitch.project: Aura of Intelligence (human-centred AI assistant) or a civic-tech pilot
pitch.framing: An Australian-built, privacy-first personal AI platform ready to test a specific overseas market and channel.
pitch.next_step: Pick one target hub (Singapore for Asia civic/health, SF for AI), draft the expansion plan, submit the online expression of interest.

name: Start-Up Chile
status: Recurring equity-free accelerator, batch-based
opens: Batch calls each year, verify next call at source
eligibility: Founders worldwide, various tracks (Build, Ignite, Growth) by stage
requires_local: Requires forming or operating a Chilean entity during the program and time in Chile
funding_amount: Roughly CLP 15-75M equity-free (about A$25k-A$115k) depending on track
other_benefits: Work visa support, soft-landing, mentor network, Latin American market access
matching_required: No (some tracks require the program to be a share of activity)
key_documents: Pitch, team, product, traction, use-of-funds
project_match: Aura assistants, civic-tech, climate/island systems
why_relevant: One of the most founder-friendly government programs on earth: real equity-free cash, open to foreigners, apply before incorporating. A genuine relocation-optional treasure.
risks: Requires meaningful time in Chile and local incorporation during the program; batch timing may not suit
verify: Confirm current track amounts, residency-days requirement and next batch dates

pitch.project: Aura personal AI or a decentralised civic infrastructure prototype
pitch.framing: A working prototype with a clear Latin American public-sector or community use case.
pitch.next_step: Watch the next Build/Ignite call, prepare a 3-minute demo and a Chile pilot idea.

name: Start Qatar
status: Foreign-founder attraction and soft-landing program
opens: Verify current intake at source
eligibility: International startups relocating or establishing in Qatar; tech and priority sectors
au_eligible: Yes in principle, subject to sector fit
requires_local: Establishing a Qatari presence is typically expected as part of the package
funding_amount: Package of incentives (varies): incorporation support, residency, potential funding access
support_type: In-kind support, incorporation and residency package, funding access
other_benefits: Residency, subsidised setup, market access, government and corporate introductions
key_documents: Company profile, business plan, sector alignment case
project_match: Smart-community and civic AI, health, immersive media
why_relevant: The named example in your brief and a strong template for Gulf soft-landing packages: setup support, residency and access rather than one small grant.
risks: Expects establishing a local presence; sector priorities and terms need direct confirmation
verify: Confirm current Start Qatar intake, exact incentives and any relocation expectation

pitch.project: Smart-community / decentralised municipal AI infrastructure
pitch.framing: A digital-noticeboard and civic AI stack ready to pilot in a fast-building smart city.
pitch.next_step: Contact Invest Qatar / QDB soft-landing team with a one-page sector-fit brief.

name: Hub71
status: Recurring incentive program with cohort intakes
opens: Cohort applications open periodically, verify at source
eligibility: Tech startups relocating to Abu Dhabi; specialist tracks (AI, ClimateTech, Digital Assets)
requires_local: Yes, establish in Abu Dhabi and locate founders there
funding_amount: Incentive package covering housing, office and insurance; potential access to capital
equity: None for the incentive itself; investment is separate
other_benefits: Subsidised housing, office and health insurance, licence support, VC network, cloud credits via partners
key_documents: Pitch deck, team, product, relocation intent
project_match: Generative AI, climate tech, Web3 / Sensorium, health
why_relevant: Substantial practical value (housing, office, insurance, compute) for a founder willing to base part of the year in Abu Dhabi. Specialist AI and ClimateTech tracks fit several projects.
risks: Requires relocation and local incorporation; cost of living high without the subsidy; cohort competition
verify: Confirm current cohort track, incentive specifics and founder-presence requirement

pitch.project: Aura generative-AI / digital-twin platform or Web3 Sensorium
pitch.framing: A privacy-preserving AI and decentralised-data platform seeking a well-resourced base in a pro-AI jurisdiction.
pitch.next_step: Target the AI or Digital Assets track; prepare deck and a clear reason to base in Abu Dhabi.

name: French Tech Ticket and La French Tech Visa
org: La French Tech (French Government) / Station F
status: Recurring visa pathway plus incubation, program specifics evolve
opens: French Tech Visa is rolling; incubator batches vary, verify at source
closes: Rolling for visa; batch-based for incubation
eligibility: Foreign founders, talent and investors; visa via recognised incubators or company track
requires_local: Residency in France for the visa; incubation may require a French base
funding_amount: Visa and incubation access; some programs historically offered stipends and grants (verify)
equity: None for the visa; incubator terms vary
other_benefits: Fast-track founder visa, Station F ecosystem, EU market access
key_documents: Project summary, incubator endorsement, proof of resources
project_match: Creative tech, AI assistants, immersive media, civic-tech
why_relevant: A credible EU relocation pathway with the world's largest startup campus (Station F) and a real founder visa. Strong for creative-tech and AI.
risks: Best value needs a French base; grant components have changed over time and must be confirmed
verify: Confirm current French Tech Visa route, any active French Tech Ticket cash and endorsing incubators

pitch.project: Immersive storytelling / creative-tech or an AI-assistant venture
pitch.framing: A European launchpad for an interdisciplinary AI-and-culture venture aiming at the EU market.
pitch.next_step: Apply to a French Tech Visa endorsing incubator (Station F programs) with a Europe rationale.

name: Estonia Startup Visa and e-Residency
status: Ongoing, always-open pathways
opens: Rolling
eligibility: Non-EU founders of scalable, innovative, tech startups
requires_local: e-Residency lets you run an EU company remotely; the visa requires relocation to Estonia
stage: Concept to growth (must pass a startup-committee innovation check)
funding_amount: No direct cash; enables low-cost EU incorporation and market access
support_type: Residency pathway and digital company formation
other_benefits: Fully digital EU company via e-Residency, low admin, access to EU grants once incorporated
key_documents: Startup description, founder background, innovation case for the committee
project_match: Privacy-preserving data systems, Web3 / Sensorium, digital-twin platforms
why_relevant: The cheapest, most digital way to hold an EU entity without leaving Australia, then unlock EU grant eligibility. e-Residency is genuinely relocation-optional.
risks: e-Residency alone is not tax residency and not a visa; committee must judge the idea as an innovative startup
verify: Confirm current startup-committee criteria and e-Residency fees

pitch.project: Privacy-preserving personal data system or Web3 Sensorium
pitch.framing: A sovereignty-of-data platform incorporated as a lean EU digital company.
pitch.next_step: Apply for e-Residency, then form an OU to become eligible for EU and NGI grants.

name: Portugal StartUP Visa and Tech Visa
city_region: Lisbon, Porto and certified incubators nationwide
status: Ongoing residency pathway via certified incubators
opens: Rolling applications via certified incubators
eligibility: Non-EU founders with an innovative project and a certified host incubator
requires_local: Residency in Portugal and a certified incubator host
funding_amount: No direct cash; residency plus incubation; competitive cost base and EU access
other_benefits: EU residency, low-cost lifestyle, strong startup community, EU grant eligibility once established
key_documents: Innovation plan, incubator endorsement, financial means
project_match: AI assistants, creative and immersive media, climate/island systems
why_relevant: A relaxed, affordable EU base for part of the year with a genuine founder visa and a vibrant community. Good bridge to EU funding.
risks: No cash component; requires a certified incubator to host the application
verify: Confirm current certified incubator list and residency conditions

pitch.project: Any early Aura or creative-tech concept
pitch.framing: An interdisciplinary founder seeking an affordable EU base to build and access European funding.
pitch.next_step: Shortlist certified incubators, secure a host endorsement, lodge the StartUP Visa application.

name: EntrePass and Startup SG Founder
status: Ongoing pass plus grant program via Accredited Mentor Partners
opens: Rolling via Accredited Mentor Partners
eligibility: Foreign entrepreneurs (EntrePass); Startup SG Founder grant favours Singapore-incorporated first-time founders
au_eligible: EntrePass yes; the founder grant typically needs local incorporation and majority local shareholding (verify)
requires_local: Grant requires Singapore incorporation; pass supports relocation
funding_amount: Startup SG Founder grant historically up to S$50k with mentorship (verify current)
other_benefits: Asia hub access, deep investor and corporate ecosystem, government tech-pilot appetite
matching_required: Founder equity/effort expected; verify co-funding terms
key_documents: Business plan, innovation case, mentor-partner engagement
project_match: Health/ageing tech, smart communities, civic AI
why_relevant: A serious Asia base with real grant money and a government keen on public-sector tech pilots. Strong springboard for health and smart-community work.
risks: Best grant value tied to local incorporation and shareholding rules; competitive assessment
verify: Confirm current Startup SG Founder amount, eligibility and shareholding conditions

pitch.project: Dementia-support / preventative-health digital twin or smart-community stack
pitch.framing: An ageing-population health platform aligned to Singapore's smart-nation priorities.
pitch.next_step: Engage an Accredited Mentor Partner and scope the local-incorporation path.

name: Start-up Visa Program
status: Ongoing permanent-residency pathway (subject to annual caps)
opens: Rolling, subject to intake caps, verify at source
eligibility: Founders with a qualifying business supported by a designated VC, angel group or incubator
requires_local: Leads to Canadian permanent residency; requires a designated-organisation commitment
funding_amount: No direct government cash; the designated organisation may invest; PR is the core benefit
support_type: Residency pathway plus potential private investment
equity: Depends on the designated backer (often equity)
other_benefits: Permanent residency, strong AI research ecosystem (Toronto, Montreal, Vancouver)
ip_conditions: You keep your IP; investor terms vary
matching_required: Needs a Letter of Support from a designated organisation
key_documents: Letter of Support, business plan, language test, settlement funds
project_match: AI research-heavy ventures, health, climate
why_relevant: A rare startup route straight to permanent residency in a top AI-research country. Best if a long-term North American base appeals.
risks: Requires securing a designated backer first; capped intake; genuine relocation
verify: Confirm current cap status, designated-organisation list and processing times

pitch.project: Deep AI cognitive-architecture venture
pitch.framing: A research-driven AI company that fits Canada's AI-institute ecosystem.
pitch.next_step: Pitch a designated incubator or angel group for a Letter of Support.

name: Global Talent Visa (Digital Technology)
status: Ongoing endorsement-based visa
opens: Rolling
eligibility: Leaders or promising talent in digital technology, endorsed by a recognised body
requires_local: Relocation to the UK; no company or job offer required
funding_amount: No cash; freedom to work, found and move without employer sponsorship
other_benefits: Self-directed UK base, path to settlement, access to UK grants and universities once resident
key_documents: Evidence portfolio, recommendation letters, endorsement application
project_match: AI, XR, civic-tech, creative technology
why_relevant: A flexible base in a major research-and-creative economy with no employer or company constraint. Your interdisciplinary portfolio suits the promising-talent route.
risks: Endorsement needs a strong evidence portfolio; no funding attached; relocation required for full value
verify: Confirm current endorsing-body criteria and evidence requirements

pitch.project: Whole portfolio, framed as a track record
pitch.framing: A multidisciplinary technologist with a body of published work across AI, XR and civic systems.
pitch.next_step: Assemble the evidence portfolio (repos, sites, letters) and target the digital-tech endorsing body.

name: Creative Destruction Lab
status: Recurring, objectives-based mentoring program with annual cohorts
opens: Annual applications, verify next cohort at source
eligibility: Seed-stage, massively-scalable science and tech companies; streams include AI and Health
au_eligible: Yes, remote participation possible for some sites
requires_local: No incorporation abroad required; some in-person sessions
stage: Seed / early with a defensible technical edge
funding_amount: No fee and no equity taken by CDL; access to world-class mentors and investors
other_benefits: Objective-setting with serial entrepreneurs and scientists, capital introductions, university research links
key_documents: Application, technical thesis, team, market
project_match: Deep AI cognitive architecture, health/dementia, privacy tech
why_relevant: Equity-free access to a serious science-and-AI mentor and investor network via universities. Strong for a technically ambitious Aura thesis.
risks: Selective and demanding; expects a scalable, science-based venture; some travel
verify: Confirm current streams, sites accepting remote founders and cohort dates

pitch.project: Aura cognitive-architecture / personal digital twin
pitch.framing: A defensible technical thesis on privacy-preserving personal AI, ready for objective-driven scaling.
pitch.next_step: Apply to the AI or Matter stream at a site that supports remote founders.

name: Horizon Europe and Marie Sklodowska-Curie Actions
status: Recurring annual calls
opens: Annual calls, verify current call at source
eligibility: Researchers and consortia; MSCA Postdoctoral and Staff Exchanges suit individuals with a host
au_eligible: Australians can participate; Australia is a strong MSCA and Horizon partner (often via a host or consortium)
requires_local: Requires a European (or associated) host institution; mobility rules apply
stage: Research-stage; needs an academic or research host
funding_amount: Salary, mobility and research costs (MSCA fellowships are well funded); consortia grants far larger
other_benefits: Salaried research placement, European lab access, publications, network
ip_conditions: IP terms set with the host; negotiable, generally researcher-respecting
matching_required: Typically no cash match for MSCA; host provides environment
key_documents: Research proposal, host agreement, CV, ethics
project_match: Hyperbaric / measured self-development research, dementia, privacy AI
why_relevant: A route to salaried, credible research funding on your health, AI and privacy questions with a European host. Turns a long-horizon idea into fundable science.
risks: Needs an academic host and formal proposal; not for a company; success rates are low
verify: Confirm current MSCA call, host eligibility and Australia participation route

pitch.project: Digitally measured self-development / hyperbaric research or privacy-AI
pitch.framing: A rigorous research question needing a European host and instrumentation.
pitch.next_step: Find a willing European lab / supervisor, co-write an MSCA Postdoctoral proposal.

name: Astera Institute Residency
status: Recurring residency for independent technologists and scientists
opens: Cohort-based, verify current call at source
eligibility: Independent researchers and builders on ambitious, high-impact public-good technology
au_eligible: Verify international eligibility; historically open to strong global applicants
requires_local: Remote-friendly; no company incorporation required
funding_amount: Salary / stipend to work full-time on the project (non-dilutive); verify current amount
other_benefits: Funded time to build, engineering and scientific community, public-good mandate
ip_conditions: Public-good / open leaning; confirm terms per residency
key_documents: Project proposal, track record, impact thesis
project_match: Aura cognitive architecture, decentralised municipal AI, privacy systems
why_relevant: Rare funding that backs an independent builder with an unconventional, long-horizon idea and no requirement to form a startup. Almost tailor-made for your Aura thesis.
risks: Very selective; public-good / openness leanings may conflict with proprietary plans; confirm eligibility for non-US applicants
verify: Confirm current residency call, stipend, IP terms and international eligibility

pitch.project: Aura cognitive architecture as public-good infrastructure
pitch.framing: An independent researcher building a privacy-first cognitive architecture as public infrastructure.
pitch.next_step: Write a crisp impact proposal and apply in the next residency window.

name: Wellcome Trust research funding (incl. Mental Health)
status: Recurring schemes across Discovery, Mental Health, Infectious Disease, Climate and Health
opens: Scheme-dependent, verify current calls at source
eligibility: Researchers, often needing an eligible host organisation; some schemes accept international applicants
au_eligible: Australian researchers can apply to many schemes, usually via an eligible host
requires_local: Host organisation usually required; not a company grant
funding_amount: Substantial multi-year research grants (scheme-dependent, can be very large)
other_benefits: Prestige, multi-year support, global health-research network
ip_conditions: Open-access and data-sharing expectations; IP terms via host
matching_required: Often no cash match; host provides environment
key_documents: Research proposal, host support, ethics, team
project_match: Dementia support, preventative health, mental health, measured self-development
why_relevant: A premier funder for your health, ageing and dementia interests, including digital mental-health tools. Turns a wellbeing idea into serious funded research.
risks: Requires an eligible research host and rigorous proposal; open-access expectations; low success rates
verify: Confirm current Mental Health / Discovery calls and host-eligibility rules for Australia

pitch.project: Dementia-support digital twin / preventative-health measurement
pitch.framing: A digitally measured, privacy-respecting approach to dementia support and prevention.
pitch.next_step: Partner an eligible research host (a QLD university) and target a Mental Health or Discovery call.

name: Dubai Future Accelerators and GovTech pilots
status: Recurring challenge-led accelerator matching startups to government entities
opens: Challenge-based cohorts, verify current calls at source
eligibility: Startups solving defined government and city challenges
requires_local: Temporary presence in Dubai during the program; pilots may lead to local setup
funding_amount: Access to paid government pilots and procurement rather than a fixed grant
support_type: Paid pilot / procurement pathway plus in-kind
other_benefits: Government customer access, real deployment sites, Dubai visibility
ip_conditions: Verify per pilot agreement; generally you keep IP
key_documents: Solution brief, challenge-fit case, demo
project_match: Smart-community, civic AI, digital noticeboards, health, climate monitoring
why_relevant: One of the clearest paths in the world to a paid government pilot for civic and smart-city tech. Turns a prototype into a real deployment and reference customer.
risks: Requires a demonstrable solution and time in Dubai; challenges are defined by government, not by you
verify: Confirm current challenge calls, entity requirements and pilot terms

pitch.project: Digital-noticeboard / decentralised municipal AI infrastructure
pitch.framing: A distributed civic-information and municipal-AI stack ready to pilot against a live city challenge.
pitch.next_step: Watch for a relevant government challenge and submit a solution-fit brief.

name: DIFC Innovation Hub and Launchpad
status: Ongoing hub with subsidised licences and programs
opens: Rolling licence and program intake, verify at source
eligibility: Fintech, AI and Web3 startups (broadening); scale and early stage
requires_local: DIFC free-zone company setup (subsidised licences available)
funding_amount: Subsidised licences, potential grants and access to capital via the ecosystem
other_benefits: Free-zone company, regulatory sandbox, corporate and VC network, visas
key_documents: Company profile, product, licence application
project_match: Web3 Sensorium, blockchain-IoT-AI integration, privacy data
why_relevant: A well-supported free-zone base for the blockchain, IoT and AI integration work, with a regulatory sandbox for data and digital-asset experiments.
risks: Requires DIFC setup and presence; strongest fit is fintech / Web3 rather than pure creative work
verify: Confirm current subsidised-licence offers and sector scope

pitch.project: Web3 Sensorium / decentralised data infrastructure
pitch.framing: A privacy-preserving decentralised-data platform seeking a sandbox-friendly free-zone base.
pitch.next_step: Enquire about a subsidised Innovation Hub licence and sandbox entry.

name: NLnet Foundation (NGI Zero funds)
status: Recurring open calls, roughly bi-monthly deadlines
opens: Rolling with regular call deadlines, verify next at source
closes: Regular fixed deadlines, verify at source
eligibility: Individuals, small teams and organisations building open, privacy-respecting internet technology
au_eligible: Yes, funds people and projects worldwide, individuals welcome
requires_local: No; fully remote, no incorporation required
funding_amount: Typically grants up to about EUR 50k (some larger), milestone-based
other_benefits: Technical and security mentoring, accessibility and standards support, EU network
ip_conditions: Requires free / open-source licensing of funded work
key_documents: Short technical proposal, budget, milestones
project_match: Privacy-preserving personal data, decentralised municipal AI, Sensorium
why_relevant: Genuinely non-dilutive, individual-friendly, remote and light to apply. Ideal for the privacy-preserving and decentralised parts of your stack. A rare grant you can win solo.
risks: Funded components must be open-source, so keep proprietary layers separate; grants are modest
verify: Confirm current NGI Zero fund themes and next deadline

pitch.project: An open, privacy-preserving component of Aura or the Sensorium
pitch.framing: A self-contained open-source module for sovereign personal data or decentralised civic infrastructure.
pitch.next_step: Carve out an open-sourceable component and submit to the next NGI Zero call.

name: Mozilla Technology Fund and awards
status: Recurring themed calls (theme changes each round)
opens: Themed annual calls, verify current theme and dates at source
eligibility: Technologists, researchers and projects advancing a trustworthy, open internet and responsible AI
requires_local: No; remote
funding_amount: Grants and awards (often five figures USD), theme-dependent
other_benefits: Open-internet community, credibility, publication and events
ip_conditions: Open / responsible-tech leaning; verify per call
key_documents: Project description, approach, impact on trustworthy tech
project_match: Privacy-preserving personal data, human-centred AI, responsible AI
why_relevant: Directly aligned with your privacy-first, human-centred AI values, and it funds mission-led builders without taking equity. Strong narrative fit for Aura's ethics.
risks: Funding follows the round's theme, which changes; must fit that year's focus
verify: Confirm the current round theme and whether your project fits it

pitch.project: Aura as human-centred, privacy-preserving AI
pitch.framing: A trustworthy-by-design personal AI that keeps data sovereign to the user.
pitch.next_step: Match Aura to the current round theme and submit a focused proposal.

name: Halcyon Incubator Fellowship
status: Recurring fellowship with residential cohorts
opens: Annual cohorts, verify current call at source
eligibility: Early-stage social entrepreneurs solving a significant social or environmental problem
au_eligible: International fellows accepted (verify current visa support)
requires_local: Residential phase in Washington D.C.; no incorporation abroad required
funding_amount: Living stipend plus residency and support (non-dilutive); verify current amount
other_benefits: Housing, stipend, mentorship, legal and strategy support, funder network
key_documents: Application, problem and solution, founder story, impact model
project_match: GAJRA Earth, civic-tech, disaster resilience, community trust
why_relevant: Backs mission-driven founders with housing and a stipend, not just mentoring. Strong for GAJRA Earth or a community-trust venture with a clear social mission.
risks: Residential phase requires time in the US; competitive; social-mission framing essential
verify: Confirm current cohort, stipend, residency length and international eligibility

pitch.project: GAJRA Earth or a community-trust / disaster-resilience venture
pitch.framing: A mission-led initiative rebuilding community trust and resilience, ready to formalise.
pitch.next_step: Craft the social-impact narrative and apply to the next fellowship cohort.

name: UNDP and UNLEASH SDG innovation programs
status: Recurring SDG innovation labs and challenges
opens: Annual talent and innovation calls, verify at source
eligibility: Young talents, innovators and teams working on the UN Sustainable Development Goals
requires_local: No incorporation; some in-person innovation-lab travel (often supported)
funding_amount: Mostly in-kind (labs, mentoring, some prizes and follow-on funding pathways)
support_type: In-kind plus challenge prizes and partner pathways
other_benefits: Global SDG network, UN visibility, partner and funder introductions
key_documents: Application, SDG-alignment case, solution outline
project_match: GAJRA Earth, climate, island resilience, sustainable community systems
why_relevant: A credible on-ramp to the UN SDG ecosystem and international partnerships for your mission-led, long-horizon work. Good for relationships and visibility even when cash is modest.
risks: Often light on direct cash; value is network and follow-on, not a big cheque
verify: Confirm current UNLEASH / UNDP calls and any prize or funding pathway

pitch.project: GAJRA Earth aligned to specific SDGs
pitch.framing: A regenerative-community initiative mapped cleanly to the SDGs, seeking global partners.
pitch.next_step: Enter the next UNLEASH talent call with a tight SDG-aligned solution.

name: GEF Small Grants Programme
org: Global Environment Facility, implemented by UNDP
country: Global (delivered through national programs, incl. Australia)
status: Recurring national grant calls
opens: Country-program calls, verify Australia / regional status at source
eligibility: Community-based organisations and NGOs on environment and sustainable development
au_eligible: Depends on the active country program; often channelled via community / NGO structures
requires_local: Community organisation or NGO vehicle usually required; local delivery
funding_amount: Typically up to about USD 50k per grant (about A$75k)
other_benefits: Technical support, global environment network, credibility for community delivery
ip_conditions: You keep your IP; community-benefit expectations
matching_required: Co-financing (cash or in-kind) is usually expected
key_documents: Community project proposal, budget, partner endorsements
project_match: Minjerribah island biosecurity, environmental monitoring, climate adaptation
why_relevant: One of the clearest routes to fund an on-island environmental or biosecurity pilot on Minjerribah through a community vehicle, with a global-standard funder behind it.
risks: Needs a community / NGO structure and co-financing; depends on the active Australian country program
verify: Confirm whether the Australian country program is active and its current call

pitch.project: Minjerribah island biosecurity and environmental-monitoring pilot
pitch.framing: A community-led, culturally appropriate environmental-monitoring project on Minjerribah.
pitch.next_step: Check the Australian GEF SGP status and line up a community organisation as the applicant.

name: Grand Challenges
status: Recurring themed open calls
opens: Multiple calls per year, verify current at source
eligibility: Researchers, innovators and organisations tackling defined global-health and development challenges
au_eligible: Yes, global applicants; some calls open to individuals and small teams
requires_local: No; remote proposals
stage: Concept (some calls fund bold early ideas at Phase I)
funding_amount: Phase I grants often around USD 100k with follow-on for success
other_benefits: Global-health network, follow-on funding, credibility
ip_conditions: Global-access conditions apply (affordable access in developing countries)

project_match: Dementia / ageing, preventative health, AI-for-health
why_relevant: Funds bold, early, unproven health ideas with real Phase I money and a path to more. Fits your dementia, ageing and AI-for-health interests.
risks: Must match a specific challenge theme; global-access IP conditions; health framing essential
verify: Confirm current open challenges and whether one fits your health work

pitch.project: AI-assisted dementia support / preventative health
pitch.framing: A bold, testable idea for measurable dementia support that could scale to low-resource settings.
pitch.next_step: Find a matching open challenge and submit a Phase I proposal.

name: Epic MegaGrants
status: Ongoing rolling program
opens: Rolling, always open
eligibility: Creators, developers, students and researchers doing notable work with Unreal Engine, 3D or the metaverse
requires_local: No; remote
funding_amount: Grants historically from about USD 5k to USD 500k, no cap stated
other_benefits: No obligation to use Epic products commercially; you keep everything you build
ip_conditions: You keep all your IP; no exclusivity
key_documents: Project description, portfolio, budget, how it advances the ecosystem
project_match: XR / spatial computing, immersive storytelling, Infinity Engine music-visuals, museums
why_relevant: Among the most founder-friendly creative grants: no equity, keep your IP, individuals eligible, up to substantial sums. Ideal for the Infinity Engine and immersive-media work.
risks: Should involve Unreal / 3D / real-time tech meaningfully; competitive at the top end
verify: Confirm the program is still accepting and current scope

pitch.project: Infinity Engine music-visuals pipeline / immersive storytelling
pitch.framing: A real-time, Unreal-powered pipeline turning music into immersive worlds for a local film community.
pitch.next_step: Package a demo reel and technical description, submit the rolling application.

name: National Geographic Society Grants

eligibility: Explorers, storytellers, scientists and educators in conservation, science, storytelling and technology

funding_amount: Level I roughly up to USD 20k; Level II larger (verify current bands)
other_benefits: Explorer network, storytelling platform, credibility, training
ip_conditions: Founder-respecting; some storytelling and sharing expectations
key_documents: Project proposal, budget, impact and dissemination plan

pitch.project: Minjerribah cultural archive / conservation storytelling (community-led)
pitch.framing: A community-led immersive record of island Country, wildlife and story.
pitch.next_step: Draft a Level I storytelling or conservation proposal with community partners.

name: Immersive festival labs (Venice, Tribeca, Sundance, SXSW)
org: Venice Biennale College Immersive / Tribeca / Sundance / SXSW
city_region: Venice, New York, Park City, Austin
status: Recurring annual selections and labs
opens: Annual submission windows, verify each festival at source
eligibility: Immersive, XR and interactive creators; some labs fund development
requires_local: No; selection-based, with travel to the festival
funding_amount: Development grants and production support (varies; Biennale College offers production funding)
other_benefits: World-stage exhibition, mentorship, distribution and festival network
key_documents: Project treatment, prototype, team, production plan
project_match: Immersive storytelling, Infinity Engine, festival and creative technology
why_relevant: Prestige routes to fund and premiere immersive work. A festival selection is both funding and a global credibility marker for your creative-tech projects.
risks: Very competitive; needs a strong prototype and treatment; travel required
verify: Confirm each festival's current immersive call, funding and deadlines

pitch.project: An immersive Infinity Engine piece or Minjerribah XR story
pitch.framing: A distinctive immersive work with a clear artistic voice and technical ambition.
pitch.next_step: Build a vertical-slice prototype and submit to the Biennale College Immersive call.

name: XPRIZE challenge competitions
status: Recurring, multiple active multi-year competitions
opens: Per-competition registration windows, verify at source
eligibility: Teams worldwide competing on defined breakthrough challenges
requires_local: No; team-based, no incorporation abroad required to register
funding_amount: Large prize purses (often USD millions across a competition) plus milestone awards
other_benefits: Global visibility, milestone funding, partner and investor attention
ip_conditions: You keep your IP (competition terms vary)
matching_required: Teams self-fund development; milestone awards help
key_documents: Registration, technical approach, milestone submissions
project_match: Climate adaptation, environmental monitoring, AI-for-good, health
why_relevant: The headline non-dilutive upside: compete on a grand challenge that matches your climate, health or AI ambitions. Even milestone stages bring funding and visibility.
risks: Long, resource-heavy commitment; winner-takes-most; needs a capable team
verify: Confirm which competitions are open for registration and their themes

pitch.project: A climate or environmental-monitoring solution (island-tested)
pitch.framing: A team tackling a defined grand challenge with a Minjerribah proving ground.
pitch.next_step: Identify an open XPRIZE that matches, assemble a team, register.

name: EIC Accelerator
status: Recurring annual cut-offs
opens: Several cut-offs per year, verify current at source
eligibility: SMEs and startups with high-risk, high-impact deep-tech innovations; EU or associated country entity required
au_eligible: Only via an EU (or associated country) entity, for example an Estonian or Portuguese company
requires_local: Yes, an EU / associated-country company is required
funding_amount: Grant up to about EUR 2.5M plus optional equity investment up to about EUR 10M (blended)
equity: Only if you take the investment component; the grant portion is non-dilutive
other_benefits: Business acceleration services, EU market access, follow-on investment
matching_required: No cash match for the grant portion
key_documents: Full proposal, business plan, pitch, due diligence
project_match: Deep-tech AI, digital twins, health, climate
why_relevant: The largest realistic non-dilutive cheque here (millions), keeping IP, if you first hold an EU entity (pair with Estonia e-Residency). A genuine growth-scale target.
risks: Requires an EU entity and a mature deep-tech case; extremely competitive and slow; not concept-stage
verify: Confirm current cut-off dates, thematic priorities and entity requirements

pitch.project: A matured Aura deep-tech component with an EU entity behind it
pitch.framing: A high-impact European deep-tech company advancing sovereign personal AI.
pitch.next_step: First establish an EU entity, build TRL evidence, then target a cut-off.

name: Protocol Labs and Filecoin developer grants
status: Recurring open grant rounds and RFPs
opens: Rolling RFPs and rounds, verify current at source
eligibility: Developers and teams building on decentralised storage, IPFS, Filecoin and open Web3 infrastructure
requires_local: No; remote
funding_amount: Grants vary widely (often five figures USD, sometimes larger for RFPs)
other_benefits: Decentralised-tech community, technical support, ecosystem exposure
key_documents: Proposal, technical plan, milestones
project_match: Web3 Sensorium, decentralised data, blockchain-IoT-AI integration
why_relevant: Non-dilutive funding for the decentralised-storage and Web3 parts of the Sensorium and personal-data sovereignty stack, with a remote, individual-friendly process.
risks: Funded work is open-source; tied to the Web3 storage ecosystem; keep proprietary layers separate
verify: Confirm current open rounds, RFP themes and terms

pitch.project: A decentralised-storage layer for sovereign personal data
pitch.framing: An open component that lets people store and control their own digital-twin data.
pitch.next_step: Match a current RFP and submit a milestone-based proposal.

name: NEOM and Saudi deep-future programs
status: Ongoing investment and partnership pathways (often bespoke)
opens: Rolling / partnership-based, verify at source
eligibility: Ventures and researchers aligned to NEOM's cognitive-city, AI, XR and sustainability agenda
au_eligible: Yes in principle, via investment or partnership routes
requires_local: Local partnership or presence typically expected for deep engagement
funding_amount: Potentially very large via investment and partnership rather than open grants
other_benefits: Cognitive-city testbed, capital scale, government backing
key_documents: Partnership proposal, capability evidence, alignment case
project_match: Smart / cognitive city, decentralised municipal AI, XR
why_relevant: A wildcard with enormous scale for cognitive-city and civic-AI ambitions if a bespoke partnership can be struck. High risk, high potential.
risks: Opaque, relationship-driven, equity / JV likely; do careful due diligence on terms and ethics
verify: Confirm any current open partnership or investment programs and terms

pitch.project: Decentralised municipal / cognitive-city AI infrastructure
pitch.framing: A distributed civic-AI operating layer for a next-generation city.
pitch.next_step: Approach only with a specific partnership concept and clear IP guardrails.

name: MassChallenge
status: Recurring annual, equity-free accelerator
opens: Annual applications, verify current cohort at source
eligibility: Early-stage startups across many sectors; various global programs
requires_local: Program-dependent; some remote participation, some hub presence
funding_amount: Zero-equity program; cash prizes awarded to top teams (varies by program)
other_benefits: Mentorship, corporate and investor introductions, prizes, alumni network
key_documents: Application, product, team, traction
project_match: Health, civic AI, climate, smart communities
why_relevant: A well-known equity-free accelerator with cash prizes and strong corporate access, open to a range of sectors and early-stage teams.
risks: Prizes go to top performers, not everyone; best programs may want hub presence
verify: Confirm which MassChallenge program suits, its location model and dates

pitch.project: A health or civic-AI venture with a clear early product
pitch.framing: An early venture with social upside and a credible team, ready to accelerate.
pitch.next_step: Pick the best-fit MassChallenge program and apply to the next cohort.

name: Techstars
status: Recurring, multiple programs with rolling and batch intakes
opens: Program-specific windows, verify at source
eligibility: Early-stage startups; many thematic and city programs
requires_local: Program is location-based; some hybrid; investment terms apply
funding_amount: Cash investment (historically around USD 120k) in exchange for equity
other_benefits: Mentor network, investor demo day, global alumni, follow-on access
ip_conditions: You keep your IP; standard investment terms
key_documents: Application, deck, team, traction
project_match: AI assistants, health, climate, civic-tech
why_relevant: A globally recognised accelerator and investor network. Included as a benchmark: strong support, but it is dilutive, so it scores lower on your non-dilutive priority.
risks: Takes equity; intensive and location-based; a big commitment for a solo founder
verify: Confirm which program fits and current terms

pitch.project: The single most investable venture in your portfolio
pitch.framing: A focused, fundable company (not the whole ecosystem) with a clear market.
pitch.next_step: Only if you want investment: pick one venture and one themed program.

name: The Earthshot Prize
status: Recurring annual nomination and prize cycle
opens: Annual nomination window via nominators, verify at source
eligibility: Solutions across five environmental Earthshots; typically nominated by official nominators
au_eligible: Yes, global; usually requires an official nominator
requires_local: No; solution and impact-based
stage: Working solution with demonstrable impact potential
funding_amount: GBP 1M per winner (five winners a year) plus finalist support
other_benefits: Global platform, network, scaling support for finalists
key_documents: Nomination, impact evidence, scaling plan
project_match: Island biosecurity, climate adaptation, regenerative community systems
why_relevant: A flagship climate prize with a life-changing purse and platform. A stretch, but a Minjerribah regenerative or biosecurity model is a genuine narrative fit.
risks: Extremely selective; usually needs an official nominator; needs demonstrated impact
verify: Confirm the current nomination route and whether direct entry is possible

pitch.project: A Minjerribah regenerative / island-resilience model
pitch.framing: A replicable island model for restoring and protecting Country.
pitch.next_step: Line up an official nominator and build the impact-evidence case.

name: Y Combinator
status: Recurring batches (twice or more per year)
opens: Batch applications, verify current at source
eligibility: Startups at almost any early stage, including idea-stage with a strong team
requires_local: In-person batch in the Bay Area; company restructures under US parent typically
funding_amount: Standard deal (historically USD 500k across two instruments)
other_benefits: Elite network, investor access at demo day, brand, alumni
ip_conditions: You keep your IP; standard investment and restructuring terms
key_documents: Application, short video, team, idea
project_match: A single high-growth AI venture from the portfolio
why_relevant: The benchmark early-stage program; accepts idea-stage with a strong founder. Dilutive and US-centred, so it scores low on your non-dilutive and low-relocation priorities, but included as a reference point.
risks: Takes equity and expects a US-parent restructure and relocation for the batch; single-company focus, not an ecosystem
verify: Confirm current batch dates and deal terms

pitch.project: One flagship AI venture, sharply defined
pitch.framing: A focused, fast-growing AI company with an exceptional founder story.
pitch.next_step: Only if pursuing venture scale: apply with one crisp company thesis.
